evidence: "If you can get by the unlikely premise, and that the Grounder women can only find low cut t-shirts in the radiated landscape of post apocalyptic Earth, it's a fun, guilty pleasure sci-fi show."
"I enjoyed watching this series, and while there are some twists and plots that have a Game of Thrones flavor, the show is different enough."
"The drama seemed overdone from time to time with closeups, quick camera shaking, and music."
"The backstory is compelling and the people all seem earnest. Nice people. But, it comes across as being somewhat scripted."
"The last season was wonderful - the tension got more intense with each episode."
"But a show that was so detailed became less and less as season went by and felt very rushed at the end."
"Production and effects were top notch, and the acting was strong."
"I enjoyed the animation, which for the most part was very well done, and having Kevin Conroy as Batman is always a plus."
"For me, BTAS is the best portrayal of Batman alongside Denny O'Neil's portrayal."
"The music was very cool -- really takes you back to the older films."
"Overall, a terrific show and great series."
"It is interesting in how each person deals with obstacles, and their success and failure."

summary: The user enjoys a mix of sci-fi and drama genres, appreciating high production quality and emotional depth in storytelling. They have mixed feelings about narrative complexity and pacing, indicating a preference for engaging plots but with some reservations about execution.
